title: Reviews - San Francisco - A Taste of SF Tours
content: > “We loved our morning tour of San Fran, Muir Woods and Sausalito. Michael was our guide and he was extremely informative about the city. Muir Woods was quite chilly in the morning but we had the perfect amount of time to walk quite a bit and visit the gift shop. It was nice and quiet in the woods. On the way back we stopped at a fort for a unique view of the bridge and had a few minutes to take pictures. > Muir Woods and Sausalito Afternoon Tour Small-Group Half-Day. My husband and I spent the weekend in San Francisco and wanted to explore outside of the city. We chose the Muir Woods & Sausalito + Half-Day Wine Country Tour, and it exceeded our expectations! The beautiful views near the Golden Gate Bridge, walking the trails among the gorgeous trees of Muir Woods, visiting the beautiful city of Sausalito, and the unique wine tasting experiences in Sonoma made for an unforgettable day.

title: San Francisco, CA: All You Must Know Before You Go (2026)
content: # Plan Your Trip to San Francisco: Best of San Francisco Tourism. # San Francisco, California. ## Essential San Francisco. ## How to do San Francisco in 3 days. ## The best cocktail bars and breweries in San Francisco. ## San Francisco is known for. ## San Francisco Travel Guide. Although the city is relatively small geographically, because of the hills, you need to buy a one, three, or seven-day visitor pass at either the airport or any Walgreens. San Francisco is a great city for walking but also has easily manageable public transportation to help you get around. San Francisco is a small city representing every culture available with the ocean to one side and the bay to the other. Greyhound buses to San Francisco are available to/from many U.S. cities, while Megabus is available to/from a few select cities in California. For more info on getting to San Francisco, visit here.

title: San Francisco Tour Blog - SF EXCURSIONS
content: | SF Excursions Wins Tripadvisor Travelers’ Choice Award 2024 7/8/2024  0 Comments    Picture  SF Excursions Recognized as a Traveler-Favorite  Tour. SAN FRANCISCO, CA – July 9, 2024 – SF Excursions is pleased to announce today that it has been recognized in Tripadvisor’s® Travelers’ Choice® Awards for 2024. This award is based on genuine feedback from anyone in the community who has visited and left an authentic, first-hand review on Tripadvisor over a 12-month period, making it a valuable and trustworthy designation of great places to visit. We are deeply grateful for your support and trust.”  “Congratulations to SF Excursions on its recognition in Tripadvisor’s Travelers’ Choice Awards for 2024,” said John Boris, Chief Growth Officer at Tripadvisor. We hope this recognition continues to drive business to you in 2024 and beyond.”  Check out all the reviews and discover more about SF Excursions here:   About SF Excursions SF Excursions offers personalized and immersive tours of San Francisco, including iconic landmarks like the Golden Gate Bridge, Muir Woods, and Sausalito.

title: 36 Hours in San Francisco: Things to Do and See - The New York Times
content: People sit on a green lawn in front of a bay with a large red bridge and mountains in the background. A frequent contributor to the New York Times Travel section, Freda Moon was born and raised in Northern California and now lives on a boat in San Francisco Bay with her husband, two kids and pet seagull, Broken Wing. But San Francisco is as beautiful as ever, developing more enticing public parks and green spaces, creating entire neighborhoods from whole cloth, and expanding arts institutions — including the new Institute of Contemporary Art San Francisco, which moved to a significantly larger space downtown in October. * Visit the **Presidio**, a national park and historic military base, and its new **Tunnel Tops** Park, with spectacular views of the Golden Gate Bridge and San Francisco Bay. * **San Francisco Maritime National Historical Park Visitor Center** offers a nuanced take on the history of the city’s waterfront. * **Anchor Oyster Bar** serves some of San Francisco’s best cioppino (a fisherman’s stew) in the Castro District, one of the city’s iconic neighborhoods.
